code.description:
A unique identifier for the type of error.

This should be a short, machine-readable string that can be used
by clients to programmatically handle different error types.
Examples: "NOT_FOUND", "VALIDATION_ERROR", "TIMEOUT"

httpStatusCode.description:
HTTP status code for this error.

Used when converting the error to an HTTP response. If None, falls back to
the error type's default status code or 500.

internal.description:
Indicates if this is an internal error that should not be exposed to users.

When `true`, this error contains sensitive information or implementation
details that should not be shown to end-users. Such errors should be
logged for debugging but replaced with generic error messages in responses.

message.description:
Human-readable error message.

This message should be clear and actionable for developers or end-users,
providing context about what went wrong and potentially how to fix it.

retryable.description:
Indicates whether the operation that caused the error should be retried.

When `true`, the error is transient and the operation might succeed
if attempted again. When `false`, retrying the same operation is
unlikely to succeed without changes.

oneOf.description:
Canonical error container that provides a structured way to represent errors
with rich metadata including error codes, human-readable messages, context,
and chaining capabilities for error propagation.

This struct is designed to be both machine-readable and user-friendly,
supporting serialization for API responses and detailed error reporting
in distributed systems.

code.description:
A unique identifier for the type of error.

This should be a short, machine-readable string that can be used
by clients to programmatically handle different error types.
Examples: "NOT_FOUND", "VALIDATION_ERROR", "TIMEOUT"

httpStatusCode.description:
HTTP status code for this error.

Used when converting the error to an HTTP response. If None, falls back to
the error type's default status code or 500.

internal.description:
Indicates if this is an internal error that should not be exposed to users.

When `true`, this error contains sensitive information or implementation
details that should not be shown to end-users. Such errors should be
logged for debugging but replaced with generic error messages in responses.

message.description:
Human-readable error message.

This message should be clear and actionable for developers or end-users,
providing context about what went wrong and potentially how to fix it.

retryable.description:
Indicates whether the operation that caused the error should be retried.

When `true`, the error is transient and the operation might succeed
if attempted again. When `false`, retrying the same operation is
unlikely to succeed without changes.

oneOf.description:
Canonical error container that provides a structured way to represent errors
with rich metadata including error codes, human-readable messages, context,
and chaining capabilities for error propagation.

This struct is designed to be both machine-readable and user-friendly,
supporting serialization for API responses and detailed error reporting
in distributed systems.

repo.description: The name of the git repository

rootDirectory.description: The name of a directory or relative path to the source code of your project. When null is used it will default to the project root

displayName.description:
Human-friendly display name for help banners and about text (e.g., "Acme Deployment CLI")

This is shown in help text and CLI output for better branding.

# Example
```text
"Acme Corporation Deployment CLI"
```

name.description:
Binary name displayed in help and usage (e.g., "my-cli")

This should be a valid CLI command name without spaces or special characters.

# Example
```text
"acme-deploy"
```

enabled.description: Whether CLI package generation is enabled

cli.description: CLI package configuration. If null, CLI packages will not be generated.

enabled.description: Whether CloudFormation package generation is enabled

cloudformation.description: CloudFormation package configuration. If null, CloudFormation packages will not be generated.

chartName.description:
Chart name (e.g., "acme-operator")

This is the name of the Helm chart and should follow Helm naming conventions.

# Example
```text
"acme-operator"
```

cliName.description:
CLI binary name to use in the operator

This should match the CLI name from ProjectCliConfig.
The operator will run `{cli_name} operate`.

# Example
```text
"acme-deploy"
```

description.description:
Human-friendly description of the chart

This appears in Chart.yaml and helps users understand what the operator does.

# Example
```text
"Acme Kubernetes Operator for managing deployments"
```

enabled.description: Whether Helm chart package generation is enabled

helm.description: Helm chart package configuration. If null, Helm packages will not be generated.

providerName.description:
Terraform provider name (e.g., "acme")

This controls how the provider is referenced in Terraform configuration.
Should be short and clean, following Terraform naming conventions.

# Example
```text
"acme"  // → provider "acme"
```

resourceType.description:
Terraform resource type name (e.g., "agent", "deployment")

This controls the resource type name in Terraform.
Common options: "agent", "stack", "environment", "deployment", "application"

# Example
```text
"agent"  // → acme_agent
```

enabled.description: Whether Terraform provider package generation is enabled

terraform.description: Terraform provider package configuration. If null, Terraform packages will not be generated.

packagesConfig.description: Configuration for embedded packages (CLI, CloudFormation, Helm, Terraform)

pullRequestUrl.description: URL to the pull request with the Alien build workflow

workflowUrl.description: URL to the GitHub Actions workflow

get.description: Get current authenticated principal information (user or service account).

Get deployment information for an agent. This endpoint requires an agent-scoped API key and returns the minimal information needed to display the deployment page.
